mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate is inversely related to equity valuations via the discount rate channel. Higher rates reduce the present value of future earnings and increase the cost of capital, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon. This relationship operates continuously across all regimes, though magnitude may vary.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent 20-day returns is above -0.03 (i.e., not negative enough) or Spearman correlation p-value > 0.05 over any consecutive 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deterioration in the labor market that reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month. The signal activates when the unemployment rate rises by at least 0.3 percentage points within a three-month period.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by >= 0.3pp over the most recent 3-month period
failure_condition: After trigger, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of occurrences, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are low and the federal funds rate is also low, it indicates that monetary easing is failing to stimulate the housing sector, signaling deep structural weakness in the economy. This joint condition predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns because the typical policy transmission channel is broken.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 900 thousand units annualized and FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
regime_hint: post-financial crisis or near-zero lower bound
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST * FEDFUNDS) has a Spearman rank IC with 20-day returns less than -0.05 over any 2-year period, or the conditional return during trigger events is not negative at 90% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial contraction (industrial production index below 98), positive growth in INDPRO indicates a turnaround in manufacturing activity that boosts cyclical earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns. In other regimes the signal from INDPRO growth is mixed and not directional.
failure_condition: Within the identified regime, the correlation between INDPRO growth and subsequent 20-day returns is not significantly positive (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 3-year window, or the average return during regime in positive INDPRO growth months is not higher than during negative growth months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in real retail and food services sales signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a short-term horizon of one week. The signal activates when monthly growth in RSAFS exceeds 1%.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following trigger are not significantly positive (mean return < 0, p > 0.10) or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive employment growth and positive retail sales growth confirms that the consumer sector is in a virtuous cycle: more jobs lead to more spending, which supports further hiring. This joint expansionary signal predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and RSAFS increase month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction (joint positive growth) has a Rank IC below 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window, or the conditional 20-day return following triggers is not significantly greater than unconditional average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong year-over-year growth in industrial production indicates sustained economic expansion that translates into higher corporate earnings over the next quarter. The signal activates when INDPRO YoY growth exceeds 2%, a level associated with above-trend manufacturing output.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns after trigger are not significantly positive (mean > 0 at 95% confidence) or the proportion of positive returns is less than 60%.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment (CPI YoY > 3%), further increases in inflation force the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy aggressively, increasing discount rates and depressing equity valuations. Therefore, when inflation is already high, a rising CPIAUCSL signals negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: During trigger events, 20-day forward returns are positive more than 40% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (p < 0.05) compared to periods without trigger.
